title: A finite row-stochastic row preserves constant inputs

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If finite row weights sum to one, applying the finite row to a constant input returns that same constant.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
the finite row weights sum to one
informal.expected_conclusion: The finite row application equals the constant value.

declarations.name: finiteRowApply
declarations.statement: def finiteRowApply {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, a i * x i

declarations.name: finiteRowApply_const
declarations.statement: theorem finiteRowApply_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a : ι → Real) (u : Real) (hsum : ∑ i ∈ s, a i = 1) : finiteRowApply s a (fun _ => u) = u

tasks.input: Translate finite row-stochastic constant preservation into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteRowApply_const {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a : ι → Real) (u : Real) (hsum : ∑ i ∈ s, a i = 1) : finiteRowApply s a (fun _ => u) = u

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding finiteRowApply, factoring the constant from the finite sum, rewriting the row sum, and simplifying.
tasks.target: Unfold finiteRowApply, use Finset.sum_mul, rewrite hsum, and ring-normalize.
